---
title: CLI Reference
---

## Basic Usage

```bash
pycmdcheck [OPTIONS] [PATH]
```

**PATH**: Directory to check (defaults to current directory)

## Options

### `-c, --check`

Run only specific check(s). Can be specified multiple times.

```bash
# Run only metadata check
pycmdcheck -c metadata

# Run metadata and tests checks
pycmdcheck -c metadata -c tests
```

### `-s, --skip`

Skip specific check(s). Can be specified multiple times.

```bash
# Skip typing check
pycmdcheck -s typing

# Skip typing and linting
pycmdcheck -s typing -s linting
```

### `--fail-on`

Exit with non-zero code on specified statuses. Can be specified multiple times. Default: `error`

```bash
# Fail on errors only (default)
pycmdcheck --fail-on error

# Fail on errors and warnings
pycmdcheck --fail-on error --fail-on warning

# Fail on any issue including notes
pycmdcheck --fail-on error --fail-on warning --fail-on note
```

### `--profile`

Run a predefined set of checks. Available profiles:

| Profile | Checks |
|---------|--------|
| `minimal` | metadata, structure, license |
| `triage` | static checks only — no install, external tools, or network (for CI triage of arbitrary repos) |
| `default` | the 13 core checks |
| `pyopensci` | all 20 checks, with stricter docs settings |
| `strict` | all 20 checks at maximum strictness |

```bash
# pyOpenSci onboarding checks
pycmdcheck --profile pyopensci

# Static-only triage (safe on an un-installed repo)
pycmdcheck --profile triage
```

A profile can be combined with `--skip` to exclude specific checks.

### `--list-profiles`

List the available profiles and exit.

```bash
pycmdcheck --list-profiles
```

### `--list`

List all available checks and exit.

```bash
pycmdcheck --list
```

Output (20 checks):
```
                           Available Checks
┏━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓
┃ Name            ┃ Description                                 ┃
┡━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━╇━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┩
│ build           │ Verify package builds successfully          │
│ changelog       │ Check for changelog or news file            │
│ ci              │ Check for CI configuration                  │
│ citation        │ Check for citation file                     │
│ community       │ Check for community health files            │
│ dependencies    │ Audit declared vs. actual dependencies      │
│ docs            │ Check documentation (README, docstrings)    │
│ doctests        │ Run doctests in package modules             │
│ formatting      │ Check code formatting (ruff format/black)   │
│ imports         │ Validate package imports                    │
│ license         │ Check for license file                      │
│ linting         │ Run code linting (ruff/flake8/pylint)       │
│ metadata        │ Validate package metadata (pyproject.toml)  │
│ py_typed        │ Check for PEP 561 py.typed marker           │
│ python_versions │ Check Python version support (EOL versions) │
│ structure       │ Validate package directory structure        │
│ tests           │ Run package tests (pytest/unittest)         │
│ typing          │ Run type checking (mypy/pyright)            │
│ urls            │ Validate project URLs are reachable         │
│ version         │ Verify version consistency                  │
└─────────────────┴─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### `-v, --verbose`

Show detailed output including timing and details for each check.

```bash
pycmdcheck -v
```

### `--json`

Output results as JSON for programmatic consumption.

```bash
pycmdcheck --json
```

Example output:
```json
{
  "package_path": "/path/to/package",
  "passed": true,
  "summary": {
    "ok": 18,
    "note": 2,
    "warning": 0,
    "error": 0,
    "skipped": 0
  },
  "results": [
    {
      "name": "metadata",
      "status": "ok",
      "message": "Package metadata is valid",
      "details": ["All metadata fields present"],
      "duration": 0.001
    }
  ]
}
```

### `--no-parallel`

Run checks sequentially instead of in parallel.

```bash
pycmdcheck --no-parallel
```

### `-x, --fail-fast`

Stop after the first check whose status matches `--fail-on`.

```bash
pycmdcheck -x
pycmdcheck --fail-fast
```

### `--debug`

Enable debug logging output (useful when diagnosing check behavior).

```bash
pycmdcheck --debug
```

### `--version`

Show version and exit.

```bash
pycmdcheck --version
```

### `--help`

Show help message and exit.

```bash
pycmdcheck --help
```

## Exit Codes

| Code | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| 0 | All checks passed (no status matching `--fail-on`) |
| 1 | One or more checks failed (status matched `--fail-on`) |
| 2 | Invalid usage (e.g., an unknown check name passed to `-c`/`--check`) |

## Examples

### CI/CD Integration

```bash
# Fail on any error (default behavior)
pycmdcheck

# Stricter: fail on warnings too
pycmdcheck --fail-on error --fail-on warning

# Skip slow checks in CI
pycmdcheck -s typing

# JSON output for parsing
pycmdcheck --json > results.json
```

### Development Workflow

```bash
# Quick check during development
pycmdcheck -c metadata -c structure

# Full check before commit
pycmdcheck -v

# List what will be checked
pycmdcheck --list
```

### Using with uv

```bash
# Run via uv
uv run pycmdcheck

# With options
uv run pycmdcheck -c tests -v
```
